results.title: Blackmagic Design Media Preservation Lab
results.brief_description: The Media Preservation Lab is a purposed built public facing lab.
results.description: There are about 250,000 artefacts in the ACMI Collection. We’ve been collecting film, videogames, artworks, home movies, stills and press kits – as well as a huge range of other paraphernalia – for over 75 years, ever since ACMI was conceived as a film lending and education organisation known as the State Film Centre. Inside the Blackmagic Media Preservation Lab, our staff and collaborators are digitising our film and time-based media collections, assessing physical artefacts from our videogame collection and film donations, as well as conducting hours of quality assessment work on our born-digital works to make the collection accessible onsite and online.
description_for_label: There are about 250,000 artefacts in the ACMI Collection, which we’ve been collecting for over 75 years – ever since ACMI was conceived as a film lending and education organisation called the State Film Centre. In the Blackmagic Media Preservation Lab, we’re digitising our film and time-based media collections, assessing physical artefacts from our videogame collection and film donations, as well as making the collection accessible onsite and online.

results.title: What's Your Angle?
results.brief_description:
A 2-player cooperative puzzle-platformer with a unique twist: asymmetrical gameplay. Unlike typical co-op games where players have similar roles, here each player experiences the game from a different gravity direction and faces unique obstacles. Despite their differences, both players contribute equally to solving the puzzles.
Each character has a unique gravity (e.g., one sees the world top-down, the other as a side-view platformer), creating drastically different experiences in the same level layout.
Players must collaborate, using character-specific abilities to assist each other and solve puzzles.
Unlike games like Toodee and Topdee, which split the perspectives, this game lets both gravity perspectives exist at the same time, adding complexity and creativity to the puzzles.

results.title: The Woods
results.credit_line:
Candice Breitz
Multi-channel installation, video, colour and audio
Commissioned by ACMI + PEM (Salem)
results.material_description: Multi-channel installation, video, colour and audio
results.brief_description: The Woods is a video installation comprised of three documentaries on child film stars from the United States of America, Nigeria and India. ‘The Audition’ features Hollywood stars, ‘The Interview’ features Nollywood stars, and ‘The Rehearsal’ features Bollywood stars. The Woods was exhibited at ACMI as part of The Character exhibition in 2015.

results.title: 01 = Zero one. [Ep. 09]
results.brief_description: Cinemagazine items including: 1. On-site manufacturing of foam plastic igloos useful for emergency housing. 2. Electrical powered feeding cart for barn-housed cattle. 3. Offset dye stamping machines for postage stamps doubling the capacity of current machines. 4. Flu vaccine production. 5. Stock cars.

results.title: Hubby gets a Hobby
results.brief_description:
This short film, ‘Hubby Gets a Hobby’, was directed by Keith Greenwood and presented by KenRa Films (Kenneth Rankine). The black-and-white, silent film pokes fun at the amateur film community (which Rankine was a part of) with a story of a husband taking up amateur cinematography as a hobby, much to his wife’s displeasure. The film’s comedic narrative comes from the husband’s obsessive focus on cameras and film equipment as his wife objects to his new hobby (demonstrating now-outdated gender stereotypes). By the end of the film, he is vindicated by capturing footage of a building fire, which is used in a newsreel, and eventually his wife is seen taking up a camera as well.

The film is notable for its footage of early models of consumer cameras, including 16mm hand-wound Bell and Howell and Kodak cameras. It also includes footage of the Victorian Amateur Cine Society filming a production about Captain Cook outside Cook’s Cottage in Fitzroy Gardens. According to an opening sequence title cards, the film was awarded a prize by the Victorian Amateur Cine Society in 1947.

Kenneth Carruthers Rankine (1890 - 1968) was a keen amateur cinematographer and filmmaker. This collection of his home movies has been donated to ACMI by Kenneth’s granddaughter. The collection represents a wonderful slice of life in Australia; with contents including scenes of everyday life, major family occasions, holidays around Victoria and views of Melbourne.

results.title: Salix8 Sunset
results.brief_description: A browser-based game that involves an abstract game-world devoid of colour. The intention of the player is to solve a series of tasks and puzzles or reach distinct goals to restore colour to the game-world.

results.title: Hint Line '93
results.brief_description:
Step back to the golden era of videogame hint lines. As callers scramble for help with cryptic games, you consult The Compendium – a binder of conflicting notes, maps and corporate propaganda. This visual novel blends digital play with physical objects: sit at the 90s-style desk, flip through the compendium,
and solve puzzles on a CRT monitor.

results.title: ACMI Egg
results.brief_description: An egg has hatched at ACMI and now a creature lives here. It needs you to feed it, care for it and keep it company.

results.title: Visitors in time
results.brief_description: Explores the way of life of the inhabitants of the former Portuguese island of Timor in the Malay archipelago. Shows the various ways in which these islanders earn a living, highlights the similarities between an ancient people and their present day descendants.

results.title: Rainy season
results.brief_description: In this video diary style film, Kuchar lets go of his usual comic persona to explore “the depression inherent to festive occasions.” From Thanksgiving dinner with friends, to a student film night disrupted by a riot, to a solemn conversation with a lover. Kuchar demonstrates the melancholy that accompanies his day-to-day routine in the rainy California holiday season.
